task: T33 — CI pipeline (.github/workflows/ci.yml + local mirror), GC/alloc perf pass (bench-as-test harnesses + reports), and packaging/dedicated-server distribution (export_presets.cfg, dist/run scripts, ADR). Owns repo-root build config, ci/, tools/*, export presets, packaging scripts, ADR docs. No gameplay/runtime edits.
baseBehaviors: Base/Makefile:3-4 — CLIENTBIN ?= xonotic-sdl, SERVERBIN ?= xonotic-dedicated: the upstream distribution is exactly TWO binaries sharing one data dir. This is the packaging topology the port mirrors (desktop client + dedicated server), per ADR-0012.

Base/Makefile:89-93 — `make server` builds darkplaces via `sv-release` then copies source/darkplaces/darkplaces-dedicated → xonotic-dedicated; Base/Makefile:96-100 — `make client` via `sdl-release` → xonotic-sdl. d0_blind_id crypto is statically linked (Makefile:80-86, DP_LINK_CRYPTO=static).

Base/Makefile:67-73 — update-stable/update-beta delegate to misc/tools/rsync-updater/*.sh: upstream's auto-update channel is rsync, NOT an installer. Precedent for the cut list: the port defers auto-update entirely.

Base/xonotic-linux-dedicated.sh is a symlink to Base/xonotic-linux-sdl.sh; the script derives mode from $0 (`case "${0##*/}" in *dedicated*) mode=dedicated`), `cd`s to its own directory first (lines 3-7 — the working-directory contract: data is found relative to the install dir), and prefers a locally-built `xonotic-$mode` over the prebuilt `xonotic-linux64-$mode`. This is the reference shape for tools/run-dedicated.sh.

Base/darkplaces/host.c:437-438 — `if (Sys_CheckParm("-dedicated") || !cl_available) cls.state = ca_dedicated;`: dedicated is the SAME binary/host loop with the client side gated off, decided at boot. host.c:543 — `if (cls.state == ca_dedicated || Sys_CheckParm("-listen"))` starts the server; host.c:652-653 — the dedicated frame returns sv_wait (server-only timing); host.c:127-128 — Host_Error on a dedicated server exits the process (no recovery screen). The port equivalent today is `--headless --host` (a listen server with a dummy renderer), not a true client-less host — acceptable v1, noted as a deferred seam.

No QuakeC source exists for CI/perf/packaging (TODO.md:985-989 confirms: 'Largely NOT a code-port task'); Base/darkplaces/.travis.yml exists but targets the C engine and is irrelevant to the C#/Godot port. CI design is therefore unconstrained by parity — only by the port's own build reality.
portState: NOTHING exists yet: no .github/, no ci/ dir, no export_presets.cfg (and .gitignore explicitly ignores `export_presets.cfg` + `export.cfg` under the '# Godot 4' block — must be un-ignored to commit presets), no packaging or run scripts. Hosted remote EXISTS: origin = git@github.com:bryankruman/XonoticGodot.git, branch `main` — so .github/workflows/ci.yml is immediately push-ready.

Build reality: XonoticGodot/XonoticGodot.csproj:1 `Sdk="Godot.NET.Sdk/4.6.3"`, net8.0, Compile Remove for src/tests/planning/.godot/obj/bin (csproj:13-20); 6 plain Microsoft.NET.Sdk class libs under src/ (only extra nuget dep: Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp 4.8.0 in SourceGen); global.json sdk 8.0.0 rollForward latestMajor (dev machine runs 9.0.308 per RUNNING.md:15); nuget.config:8 adds local source `C:\Program Files\Godot\GodotSharp\Tools\nupkgs` (verified: contains Godot.NET.Sdk.4.6.3.nupkg, GodotSharp.4.6.3.nupkg, Godot.SourceGenerators.4.6.3.nupkg).

KEY VERIFIED FACT that dissolves the Godot-SDK-in-CI problem: nuget.org NOW HAS Godot.NET.Sdk 4.6.3 and GodotSharp 4.6.3 (live flat-container query, versions list includes "4.6.3") — the nuget.config:3-5 / csproj:4-5 comments ('the stable 4.6.3 SDK isn't on public NuGet') are STALE. CI can restore everything from nuget.org; the only hazard is the dangling Windows-only local source on a Linux runner (see risks).

Tests verified green NOW (uncommitted player-loop tree): `dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/XonoticGodot.Tests.csproj -c Debug` → Passed 1159, Skipped 1, Total 1160, Duration 11 s. tests csproj is plain Microsoft.NET.Sdk (xunit 2.9.2, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.11.1) — no Godot dependency, runs on any runner. TestParallelization.cs disables xUnit parallelism assembly-wide (global-state safety; suite is fast anyway).

~18 test files hardcode `C:\Users\Bryan\Projects\Xonotic\XonoticGodot\assets\data` but ALL skip gracefully when missing (`if (!Directory.Exists(DataDir)) return;` — e.g. BspCollisionTests.cs:276, AssetParserTests.cs:22, BotPerfBench.cs:43) — so CI without assets passes, silently losing the real-data assertions. Acceptable v1; an XG_DATA_DIR env override is a T31-adjacent improvement (tests/* is T31's wave file).

Headless/CLI flags (Main.cs:57-127): --net-loopback (in-process server+bot+client), --map <vpath>, --gametype <short>, --menu-screen <id>, --connect <addr>, --host [map] (listen server + self-connected client), --bots N, --screenshot <path> + --screenshot-frames N (WINDOWED only — headless renders blank, Main.cs:113/RUNNING.md:188-191). GameDemo adds --fx-demo [effect] and --proj-demo (GameDemo.cs:222-230, 702-713). NOTE: `--dbg-fwd` (from session memory) is ABSENT in the tree — grep finds nothing; treat as removed/dead.

Runtime launch reality (RUNNING.md:12-13): Godot 4.6.3 mono console exe at `C:\Program Files\Godot\Godot_v4.6.3-stable_mono_win64_console.exe`; headless smoke = `"$GODOT" --headless --path <root> --quit-after 200` with grep for `^ERROR:|SCRIPT ERROR|Unhandled exception` (RUNNING.md:56-86 has the exact copy-paste one-liner + expected output). dotnet build of the host outputs to .godot/mono/temp/bin (RUNNING.md:156).

NO Godot export templates installed on the dev machine (~/AppData/Roaming/Godot/export_templates does not exist) — no export has ever been attempted; export_presets.cfg never existed. The export half of packaging is fully greenfield and untested.

Perf infra that already exists: tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/BotPerfBench.cs — the canonical bench-as-test pattern ([Fact] + Stopwatch + GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread at :193-206, asset-skip guard at :43, report to temp file + ITestOutputHelper, run via `dotnet test --filter BotPerfBench -l "console;verbosity=detailed"`); tools/loc.py (LOC report incl. baked Base baseline); tools/movement-ref/ (C golden-trace generator, binary gitignored). The T33 bot-nav perf pass already LANDED (TODO.md:991-1002: graph build 219→8 ms, Nearest 29×, Think 0.039 ms/bot/tick).

Alloc state of the named hot-path candidates — mostly ALREADY pooled, so the pass must be measurement-first, not assumption-driven: SnapshotDelta.cs:100-106 ring-slot dict reuse ('no per-tick allocation') + :158-161 client-side same; ServerNet.cs reuses _snapshotWriter (:770 Reset) + _entityScratch (:834-836 'Reused dictionary — no per-tick alloc'); NetTransport.cs:101-103 PutPacket ReadOnlySpan overload (no managed copy); TraceService.cs:56 single reused _candidates List<Brush>(64). Remaining measurable costs: O(clients×entities) baseline-dict copy per tick (SnapshotDelta.cs:104-106), NetEntityState.Diff over every entity every tick (SnapshotDelta.cs:91), per-trace interface dispatch TryGetEntityBrushModel (TraceService.cs:140,200).

Asset/distribution flow: download-assets.sh sits at REPO ROOT (its usage comment './tools/download-assets.sh' is stale) — clones xonotic-data/music/maps pk3dirs from gitlab.com/xonotic (pruning qcsrc etc., DATA_EXCLUDE_DIRS line 33), sparse-checkouts 4 font pk3dirs, and extracts 2 compiled-map pk3s from the ~960 MB dl.xonotic.org/xonotic-0.8.6.zip; writes assets/.gdignore so Godot's importer skips the tree. .gitignore keeps `assets/*` (except .gdignore). Exported-build data resolution: GameDemo.ResolveDataPath (GameDemo.cs:370-391) maps res://assets/data via ProjectSettings.GlobalizePath("res://") — which returns "" in an exported build → Path.GetFullPath resolves CWD-relative `assets/data`. So the packaging contract is: assets/data beside the binary + run scripts cd to the install dir (exactly the Base xonotic-linux-sdl.sh shape).

ADR numbering: planning/decisions/ has ADR-0001..0013; next free is ADR-0014. ADR-0012 sanctions Windows/Linux/macOS desktop clients + headless dedicated server, defers web/mobile; it already names the dedicated-server export (`--headless`, dedicated_server feature tag, resource stripping) as the server topology.
plan: 1. CI workflow — create .github/workflows/ci.yml (push/PR on `main`). Job `test` (ubuntu-latest, ~2 min): actions/checkout → actions/setup-dotnet (9.0.x; global.json rollForward latestMajor accepts it) → `dotnet nuget remove source godot-editor --configfile nuget.config` (neutralizes the Windows-only local source so both MSBuild SDK resolution and restore use nuget.org, where Godot.NET.Sdk 4.6.3 verifiably exists) → `dotnet restore tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/XonoticGodot.Tests.csproj` → `dotnet test -c Debug --no-restore --logger "trx;LogFileName=test-results.trx" --results-directory TestResults` → actions/upload-artifact TestResults (always()). Job `build-host` (ubuntu-latest, same nuget step): `dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj -c Debug` — proves the Godot host compiles from a clean clone with zero Godot editor installed. Real-data tests self-skip without assets (by design).

2. Optional `export` job in the same yml, gated `on: workflow_dispatch` + tags (NOT per-push — the mono export-templates tpz is ~1 GB): download Godot_v4.6.3-stable_mono_linux_x86_64.zip + Godot_v4.6.3-stable_mono_export_templates.tpz (actions/cache keyed '4.6.3-stable-mono'), unpack templates to ~/.local/share/godot/export_templates/4.6.3.stable.mono/, `godot --headless --path . --import`, then `--export-release "windows-client"` and `--export-release "linux-dedicated"`, upload dist artifacts. Mark continue-on-error: true for the first iterations (export has never run anywhere).

3. Local CI mirror — ci/ci.sh (git-bash compatible, absolute paths, no cd chains): step 1 `dotnet build tests/...csproj -c Debug`; step 2 `dotnet test` (expect 1159+/1160, 11 s); step 3 `dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj -c Debug`; step 4 (if "$GODOT" exists) the RUNNING.md:66-70 headless smoke one-liner asserting `hard errors: 0`; step 5 (if export templates installed) the two export-release calls. Optionally ci/ci.ps1 thin wrapper. On dev machines the godot-editor nuget source exists so no remove-source step locally.

4. Packaging presets — create export_presets.cfg with two presets: [preset.0] name="windows-client", platform="Windows Desktop", runnable=true, export_filter="all_resources", binary x86_64, embed_pck=true; [preset.1] name="linux-dedicated", platform="Linux", export_filter="dedicated_server" (Godot 4's 'Export as dedicated server' mode — strips visual resources + applies the dedicated_server feature tag ADR-0012 names). Remove the `export_presets.cfg` line from .gitignore (keep `export.cfg`; no secrets — desktop presets carry no keystores).

5. Packaging assembly — tools/package.sh: takes the two export outputs + an assets dir (reuses download-assets.sh if absent; supports --no-music), lays out dist/windows-client/{XonoticGodot.exe,assets/data/} and dist/linux-dedicated/{xonoticgodot-dedicated.x86_64,assets/data/}, zips both. tools/run-dedicated.sh mirroring Base/xonotic-linux-sdl.sh: resolve+cd to script dir (the ResolveDataPath CWD contract, GameDemo.cs:370-391), exec `./xonoticgodot-dedicated.x86_64 --headless --host "${1:-stormkeep}" --gametype "${GAMETYPE:-dm}" "$@"`. Windows client needs no script (assets/data sits beside the exe; double-click works only if launched from its own dir — document, and note the --data seam below).

6. Perf pass (measurement-first; all headless `dotnet test` benches, new additive files under tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/Perf/, BotPerfBench pattern: Stopwatch + GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread + skip-if-no-assets + env-var XG_DATA_DIR with the hardcoded path as fallback): (a) NetSnapshotPerfBench — ServerSnapshotHistory.EncodeSnapshot + ClientSnapshotHistory.DecodeSnapshot round-trip at 16 clients × 256 entities × 72×30 ticks; assert/record B/tick (expected ~0 steady-state given the ring pooling at SnapshotDelta.cs:100-106) and ms/client-tick of the Diff+dict-copy (SnapshotDelta.cs:85-106). (b) TracePerfBench — TraceService sweeps on real atelier collision (BspCollisionBuilder.Build), 10k box-sweeps of varying length; ms/trace + B/trace; isolates the TryGetEntityBrushModel dispatch cost (TraceService.cs:140,200). (c) ServerTickPerfBench — boot a GameWorld on atelier with 4 spawned players + items and run 72×20 sim ticks (tests may CALL GameWorld; no edits — it's T39's chokepoint); ms/tick + B/tick is THE dedicated-server capacity number. (d) keep BotPerfBench as the bot-nav regression baseline (0.039 ms/bot/tick). (e) map-load wall-time line inside (b) (BspReader.Read + Build) as the startup metric. Record baselines as comments in each bench + a summary in the final message for the orchestrator (findings in chokepoint files = REPORTS, not edits).

7. Live-process tooling doc — RUNNING.md 'Tricks' addition: dotnet-counters attach to the running Godot PID (gen0/gen1 rate, % time in GC) for client-side paths the headless benches can't reach (EffectSystem._Process — game/client is T48's wave file; HUD rebuilds); how to run the Perf benches (`dotnet test --filter PerfBench -l "console;verbosity=detailed"`). Explicitly do NOT flip GC mode (ServerGarbageCollection) in XonoticGodot.csproj without dotnet-counters evidence — propose-only.

8. Docs — planning/decisions/ADR-0014-ci-packaging-distribution.md: CI topology (test always / build-host always / export on-demand), the nuget.org-vs-bundled-SDK resolution story (record that 4.6.3 IS published and the editor source remains a dev-machine convenience), packaging layout + the assets-beside-binary CWD contract, dedicated-server = headless listen server for v1 (true client-less host deferred to a Shell/NetGame seam), and the explicit cut list with rationale. Fix the stale comments while touching owned files: nuget.config:3-5, download-assets.sh usage line (says ./tools/), RUNNING.md:41 stale '19 tests'. Add the CI badge to README.md.

9. Cut list (defer, with rationale in ADR-0014): installers (MSI/NSIS/deb/flatpak) — zip artifacts suffice pre-1.0; auto-update — upstream uses rsync (Base/Makefile:67-73), no release channel exists yet; macOS export — sanctioned by ADR-0012 but signing/notarization gates it, revisit at release; code signing — same; Steam/itch — out of scope; per-run CI asset download (~960 MB + multi-GB clones) — real-data tests skip by design, an asset-cached nightly job is a follow-up; BenchmarkDotNet — rejected in favor of the zero-dependency bench-as-test pattern already proven by BotPerfBench.
filesToModify: .gitignore (remove the `export_presets.cfg` ignore line; add dist/ + TestResults/)
nuget.config (fix the stale 'not on public NuGet' comment — 4.6.3 is published; keep the godot-editor source for offline dev)
RUNNING.md (CI section, dedicated-server run, perf-bench + dotnet-counters tricks; fix stale '19 tests' at line 41)
download-assets.sh (fix the stale './tools/download-assets.sh' usage comment; optional --dest flag for tools/package.sh reuse)
chokepointSnippets: NONE REQUIRED — the CI/perf/packaging deliverables need zero edits in GameWorld.cs, ServerNet.cs, ClientNet.cs, NetProtocol.cs, Commands.cs, DamageSystem.cs, MapObjectsRegistry.cs, or Registries.cs. Benches only CALL GameWorld/TraceService/SnapshotDelta read-only from tests.

OPTIONAL SEAM (Main.cs — runtime bootstrap, not owned by T33; hand to orchestrator): a `--data <path>` CLI flag makes exported builds robust when CWD ≠ install dir (ResolveDataPath falls back to CWD-relative 'assets/data' in exports, GameDemo.cs:377-384). Exact integration: in Main._Ready after the --bots block (Main.cs:98-100) add `int d = Array.IndexOf(args, "--data"); if (d >= 0 && d + 1 < args.Length) shell.DataPath = args[d + 1];` — Shell.DataPath already exists ([Export], game/Shell.cs:30) and flows into MenuState.Boot(DataPath) at Shell.cs:68. No wire/protocol change.

DEFERRED SEAM (game/Shell.cs + game/net/NetGame.cs — T39's wave files): a true client-less dedicated mode. Today `--host` (Shell.cs:115 `else if (BootHost)` → StartHost at Shell.cs:~482) always constructs a NetGame hosting server AND a self-connected local client; `--headless --host <map>` is therefore a headless listen server, which is the v1 dedicated story. A real `--dedicated` would instantiate the ServerNet/GameWorld half without the client/render half (the host.c:437 ca_dedicated analogue). Report-only this wave; record in ADR-0014.

PERF REPORT TARGETS (no edits — owned/chokepoint files): (1) game/net/ServerNet.cs snapshot send loop (:770-826) — per-client wire order is [byte NetControl.Snapshot][float now][ulong LastProcessedSeq][ushort ownerNetId][WriteOwnerState — append-at-END lockstep gotcha][bool movevars(+MoveVarsBlock)][bool scoreinfo(+ScoreInfoBlock)][bool scores(+ScoreboardBlock)][SnapshotDelta entity section: ushort snapshotSeq, ushort baselineSeq, ushort removedCount×ushort, ushort changedCount×(ushort+delta)]; the conditional blocks are already hash-gated (:780,:795,:805) and the writer/scratch are reused — any B/tick found by ServerTickPerfBench in this file goes to T39's owner as a report. (2) src/XonoticGodot.Server/GameWorld.cs tick internals — same. (3) game/client/EffectSystem.cs per-frame allocs (37 new/foreach sites) — game/client/* is T48's wave glob → dotnet-counters findings reported, not patched.

tests/* COORDINATION (T31 nominally owns tests/* this wave): the three new benches are additive files under a new tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/Perf/ subfolder — zero overlap with T31's parser/builder/NPC coverage targets, but the orchestrator should reserve the Perf/ folder name for T33. Existing BotPerfBench.cs is left untouched.
risks: nuget.config's Windows-only local source on a Linux runner: NuGet errors on a missing local folder source during restore, and the MSBuild SDK resolver for `Sdk="Godot.NET.Sdk/4.6.3"` reads the same config hierarchy. Mitigation is the explicit `dotnet nuget remove source godot-editor --configfile nuget.config` CI step BEFORE any dotnet command; verify on the first real Actions run. (Fallback: a CI-only config + `dotnet restore --configfile`, but that does not cover SDK resolution — the remove-source approach does.)

Export is 100% untested: no export_presets.cfg has ever existed and no export templates are installed on the dev machine. First-run hazards: the mono export-templates tpz (~1 GB) download/cache; cross-export of the C# game to Windows from a Linux runner (needs the dotnet SDK on the runner; Godot drives `dotnet publish -r win-x64`); whether `export_filter="dedicated_server"` strips something the headless server actually needs. Budget a debug iteration; keep the export job continue-on-error initially.

Exported-build asset resolution rests on the CWD contract (GameDemo.cs:377 GlobalizePath("res://") == "" when exported → 'assets/data' resolved against CWD). If a user launches the exe from elsewhere the mount silently fails (game boots to a blank/flat world — RUNNING.md:179-184's '0 missing cvars' signal inverts). Run scripts cd first (Base parity), but the durable fix is the Main.cs --data seam (chokepointSnippets) — flag to the orchestrator.

GitHub-hosted runners cannot do visual capture (--screenshot requires a windowed renderer; --headless captures blank, Main.cs:113, RUNNING.md:188-191) — CI asserts build+tests+boot-log only; visual QA stays local (T5). Do not let the smoke job grep WARNING counts without assets mounted — the expected-output baseline (RUNNING.md:73-81) assumes assets exist.

Asset volume: never download per CI run (~960 MB release zip + multi-GB git clones). The 18 real-data test files already skip without assets — CI green therefore proves LESS than a local run; record in ADR-0014 and keep the local ci.sh (with assets) as the authoritative gate. A cache-warmed nightly real-data job is deferred.

Perf-pass expectation management: the named candidates (snapshot serialization, TraceService, ServerNet) are ALREADY pooled (SnapshotDelta.cs:100-106, TraceService.cs:56, ServerNet.cs:834-836) — the pass may legitimately conclude 'measured, no action needed' for some paths; the deliverable is baselines + regression benches, not forced micro-optimizations. Conversely do NOT flip ServerGarbageCollection / GC modes in XonoticGodot.csproj without counter evidence (client frame-pause vs dedicated throughput trade-off).

Working tree has uncommitted player-loop changes across 17 files (GameWorld, ServerNet, ClientNet, etc.) — CI lands as NEW files plus 5 low-traffic modifications (.gitignore, nuget.config, RUNNING.md, README.md, download-assets.sh), so merge risk is minimal, but the first Actions run only happens after the orchestrator commits/pushes; ci.yml must be correct-by-inspection (consider running `bash -n ci/ci.sh` + a YAML parse locally as the pre-push lint).

tests/Perf/ benches that load real assets add ~seconds each to the 11 s suite on dev machines (and skip in CI). Keep tick/iteration counts modest and consider a `[Trait("Category","Perf")]`-style filter so `dotnet test` stays fast by default if duration creeps.
testPlan: Local CI mirror end-to-end: `bash ci/ci.sh` from a clean shell → libs+tests build, dotnet test reports ≥1159 passed / 0 failed (current baseline: 1159 passed, 1 skipped, 11 s), host `dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj` clean, headless smoke (`--headless --quit-after 200`) reports `hard errors: 0` per the RUNNING.md:66-70 one-liner.

Workflow correctness without a push: YAML-parse ci.yml (python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]))"), `bash -n` both scripts; then simulate the Linux nuget condition locally: `dotnet nuget remove source godot-editor --configfile nuget.config` in a scratch copy + `dotnet restore XonoticGodot.csproj` must succeed purely from nuget.org (proves the 4.6.3-on-nuget.org finding holds for the full dependency closure including Godot.SourceGenerators).

Export verification (local, Windows): download + install the 4.6.3 mono export templates, run `"$GODOT" --headless --path . --import` then `--export-release "windows-client" dist/windows-client/XonoticGodot.exe`; launch the exported exe from its own directory with assets/data placed beside it: `--map stormkeep --screenshot dist/_verify.png` and Read the PNG — proves both the export and the ResolveDataPath CWD contract in one shot.

Dedicated-server verification: export `linux-dedicated` (run under WSL) or proxy with the Windows console exe: `--headless --host atelier --bots 2 --quit-after 2000`, assert the boot banner + map-load log lines + 0 hard errors; optionally a second windowed instance `--connect 127.0.0.1` joins and renders (the existing NetGame play path).

Perf benches: `dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests --filter PerfBench -l "console;verbosity=detailed"` → each bench prints ms + B/op tables and writes its temp report; record the captured numbers as baseline comments in the bench files; assert the suite still passes WITHOUT assets present (skip guards) by temporarily pointing XG_DATA_DIR at an empty dir.

Regression guard: full `dotnet test` after all changes stays at 0 failed; `git status` shows ONLY T33-owned files touched (no chokepoint files); the final message carries the perf REPORT findings (ServerNet/GameWorld/EffectSystem numbers) for the orchestrator to route to T39/T48 owners.

Post-push (orchestrator action): first real GitHub Actions run on `main` — test + build-host jobs green, trx artifact uploaded; then a manual workflow_dispatch of the export job to shake out the templates/cross-export path.
